commit.message:
Don't leave unlinked items around after fleeing an apostle challenge

Removing the challenger as you leave the floor was unintentionally being
treated as beating them properly, scheduling a revival fineff that would
never be properly processed and generally leaving them in an invalid state
(since it happened in the middle of leaving the floor).

This would unfortunately bombard the player with constant error messages
about unlinked items every turn they spent on that floor for the rest of
the game.

Thanks for Roxie and Ogregutan for the reports. My apologies that it took
me so long to actually fix this one.
Update credits

Stop mutation catalyst rolling beaks (Emily Bunny)

These were missing from the list of banned body facet mutations.

Buff athames a bit more

While 89500f3 went through and made athames reasonably more common,
they're still relatively uncommon picks due to being worse than rapiers
without fulfilling their conditions and worse than quick blades as one
can try to hold out for finding, which hurts their design intent as
curve-smoothing items meant to support mildly more unique builds. (While
the ceiling of Short Blades characters don't need buffs, quick blades are
still fairly better than them when fulfilling their own particular needs
anyway.)

This commit bumps up athame base damage from 6 to 7, letting them be
strictly better rapiers aside from the swapping penalties and needing
slightly more skill investment, so that bothering with their debuff
conditions is more of interest. It also lets them have brands as often as
daggers do (since brand weapon scrolls are often better spent on quick
blades anyway), with mild bumps up of likelihood for flaming, freezing,
protection, devious, vampiric, and draining brands. This is still a
fairly cautious nudge up, but it should hopefully make them worth
considering more often without letting short blades characters just
ignore the more unique kinds of item / spell / god support they've
accumulated over time.

Tune down OTR, Refridge, lost soul flashes

This is another follow-up to cfa9558, using the updated functionality of
the previous commit to reduce the screen flash intensity for Olgreb's
Toxic Radiance, Ozocubu's Refrigeration, and lost souls replacing another
monster on their death. The first two effects being full screen flashes
is reasonably appropriate for their theming and fullscreen range but can
afford to be far less garishly bright and abrupt each time, and as a large
shift of colour it should still be reasonable to notice coming from
monsters. The lost soul effect should still be replaced entirely, but as
it can be adjusted with a single line change now, it is also subdued more
mildly compared to the other two.

Allow specifying alpha of screen flashes

[Committer's notes: While this adjusts the parameters passed to the flash
in most of its calls, this commit doesn't actually change the alpha of any
of these flashes and is only meant to set up the capacity to change such.
Changes to actually provide less sharp flashes will be done in the
following commit.]

A quick small set of monster spell icon updates

When looking through the icon list for components for future spells'
icons, I was stricken with inspiration for updating some of the very
old monster spell icons, which unnecessarily bled past the standard
26x26 frame. This commit adds a fair number of them, remixing other
components as I usually do:

 * Summon Eyeballs: Denzi's old great orb of eyes tile and the usual
   generic summoning sigil Ontoclasm made, as is also used with these
   other summoning spell icons added here.

 * Summon Drakes: Ontoclasm's current Dragon's Call icon.

 * Summon Scorpions: Denzi's old Summon Scorpions icon.

 * Fire Breath: Sastreii's current fire dragon tile and Denzi's old
   Breathe Fire ability icon.

 * Cold Breath: Sastreii's current ice dragon tile and ontoclasm's
   current white draconian Glacial Breath icon.

 * Spit Poison: Denzi's old Spit Poison icon and ontoclasm's current
   Spit Poison icon.

 * Steam Ball: Denzi's old Steam Breath icon and ontoclasm's current
   Fireball icon.

 * Quicksilver Bolt: SentientSupper's Bolt of Light icon and
   ontoclasm's lightning rod Thunderbolt icon.

Fix temp terrain reverting causing some doors to have the wrong tile

If multiple in view temporary terrain squares reverted at the same time
in Crypt and one of them revealed a door and another revealed terrain
that blocked sight of the door, the door would show the non-crypt tile
until the game was reloaded or the player moved so that the door was in
view.

Fix doors closed by sealing sometimes showing the wrong tile

If a large door was sealed in Crypt, any parts of the door that were out
of sight would show the non-crypt door tile until the game was reloaded
or the player moved so that they were in sight.

Fix crypt doors sometimes using non-crypt tiles

If the player opened or closed a large door in Crypt were some of the
door was out of sight, the out of sight part of the door would show the
non-crypt tile until the game was reloaded or the player moved to by
able to see that part of the door.
Remove unused MAP_EXCLUDED_STAIRS flag from map_cell

Fix walls with torches not animating in local tiles

This was broken in commit 45ea6cc when it added back some special casing
for floors and rock walls but didn't add back the special casing for
their animations.

Revert "Rework lightning rod damage"

This reverts commit d6847357b22e053b920fe82ce22be270941b2ead.

Meant to push this to a branch.

Rework lightning rod damage

The current damage from lightning rod sheet lightning is quite
counterintuitive; it does reduced damage to each enemy according to the
number of tiles hit at the same distance from the player.

This is quite hard to think about, and it means that using the lightning
rod to hit a large area is usually less effective than using it for
single beam damage. It also has fiddly targetting shenanigans where it
is good to decrease the number of squares hit.

We update this so that:
* The main beam does full damage every cast
* Squares off the main beam take half damage

This is intended to incentivise more use of AoE; as long as the player
has sensible targets for the main beam they should want to move the beam
in a wide arc to deal some damage to more targets.

Since this should be a buff for lightning rod, we nudge the single-beam
damage numbers down to compensate.

Also make the displayed damage deterministic.

0.35 changelog update (through e467b9c7cb)

Better late than never, of course.

Loose other minor tile adjustments

 * Misery clouds have had their palette compressed to fit other negative
   energy effect palettes.

 * Mlioglotl's Corruption effect has been tweaked some more, animating
   faster but with a fixed very short pause at the end, to hopefully
   continue to be less annoying as a bound soul.

 * Mild additional rltiles/unused sorting has been done, including moving
   a set of beta poltergeist effect tiles that never ended up being used
   anyway.

Add new Shatter vfx + option to choose either

Shatter's animation has been somewhat controversial over time in its
screenshake effect, especially with the way webtiles is coded versus the
other formats making it shake the slice of the screen corresponding to
the 80x24 console equivalent instead of all one sees in webtiles. This
has gone on so far that some players recommend turning off all player
effects entirely, which reasonably elicits some intervention.

As an alternative, there is now a new bespoke multiple shockwaves
animation, derived from an LRD explosion submission by LemurRobot (which
isn't implemented yet for the many special cases it'd require). One can
opt for choosing either with the new option, `alt_shatter_animation`,
which is set to true on webtiles and false on local tiles or console.
Either way, the walls actually broken by Shatter are now highlighted
after each cast, rather than them spontaneously disappearing outright.

(A third much simpler animation setting for Shatter might be considered
if people who don't turn off all animations think the new effect is
somewhat excessive for a spell cast many times in a row, but really,
level 9 spells should have _some_ fanciness to them for how long it
takes to reach them.)

Since the shillelagh "Devastator" uses the same damage evaluation as
Shatter, it now uses one of Shatter's vfx whenever it hits instead of a
generic earth / acid splash highlight, regardless of the above settings.

Replace a decent chunk of mid-combat screen flashes

Screen flashes meant to actually show up mid-combat (as opposed to effects
like god brands or rune pick-ups or opening the door to Zot) should be
reserved for fairly larger swings in combat like Fear or Paralysis or being
at fairly low health, rather than being very distracting as a default
resort for various. This commit serves as a delayed follow-up to fe3fe7f in
trying to remove various other combat flashes that can just have target
highlighting instead, to both provide more unique vfx and to help pare down
excessive screen colour flashes.

 * Leda's Liquefaction: instead of multiple brown-yellow screen flashes,
   it now highlights every square that'll have the mud appear with layered
   flickering ripples, with a new effect combining some CC0 dirt scraps by
   Surt with CC0 water-and-mud pieces by josehzz.

 * Eringya's Noxious Bog: instead of a green screen flash, it now
   highlights every square that'll have the bog appear with a brief
   couple of faint bursts, with a new faint effect combining CanOfWorms's
   irradiate explosions with the same CC0 dirt graphics by Surt that
   the bog tiles uses as of c4f44f9.

 * The Singing Sword: instead of a yellow screen flash, it now
   highlights every monster hit with a new cartoony music effect
   derived from CanOfWorms's Fugue of the Fallen spell icon. (This
   would normally be a bit out of place, but the item itself has some
   extraordinarily silly singing lines, so it felt appropriate.)

 * Slime creatures merging: instead of a green screen flash, it has
   a new pair of effects on the nearby tiles of the fused slime creature,
   reusing old slime creature tiles by roctavian. (This one is one of the
   ones I'm most worried about, but the new animation's movement display
   should still be reasonably noticeable, and slime creatures are already
   something to play cautiously and slower around anyway.)

 * Drain Life: instead of a black screen flash, it has a quick vacuum
   animation and highlights for players and monsters hit, with a new
   effect akin to Gloom's further remixing CC0 particle effects by
   Kutejnikov.

The remaining main targets for further adjustment this version are
Olgreb's Toxic Radiance and Ozocubu's Refrigeration, which probably could
just resort to fainter transparency in a future commit, and lost soul
revival, which is much rarer than these other effects and wants some
fairly bespoke effects. Of the other major mid-combat effects:

 * Maxwell's Capacitive Coupling has long intervals between each screen
   flash, so I'm not too worried about it,

 * Cause Fear and Call Tide deserve the flash as much as paralysis and its
   friends do,

 * and lurking horror explosions want some settling on Symbol of Torment
   itself having any visual effects first.

Remove spammy resists message

Following the previous commit, it has been pointed out that "X resists"
every turn is somewhat spammy. We print one resistance message anyway
when the monster is initially hit, and there is a punctuation difference
when they resist fire, so the player still gets some feedback.

We keep these messages for the player resisting burning - this is higher
signal/noise ratio. This is consistent with how we message for actors
resisting clouds.

Improve sticky flame messaging.

Add resist messages for player and monster.
Closes #3885.

Committer's note: tweaked the code for the monster versions to improve
readability.

Flip ignore_temp flags to include_temp

This makes them consistent with other such flags.

Rename boolean temp parameters to include_temp

We have many boolean parameters on play attributes that mean
"also include temporary effects". The author finds "temp" a
confusing name for these, as it reads too much like "only temp".
We rename them to "include_temp" for clarity.
Fix monster bot not handling AF_BURSTSHROOM
Fix monster bot not handling unicode monster glyphs properly

Describe attack delay changes on all items

This updates our displaying of attack delays on armour (which was
previously only for the equipped body armour on ranged weapons) to use
the same machinery as AC/EV/SH changes. This makes it display on all
items which change these delays (except weapons themselves), and to
preview changes for unequipped items.

Update @ attack delay to be rounded to the nearest integer

Missed in fe84ade

Separate hidden spells for divine exegesis

Change the divine exegesis menu so that it maintains a separate list
of hidden spells from memorisation.

The player often wants these lists to be very different, as exegesis
uses spells the player will never be able to cast.

Make stash search sort results by type, by default

The current default is distance, which can be moderately relevant when
finding distant items, but in many cases makes it harder to evalute the
results of any search that finds many items at once (especially those that
also contain features, which are irrelevant to larger searches most of the
time).

Now it sorts first by item base type instead.

In many cases, I feel this makes searches easier to parse (especially if
one wants to examine items on their current floor).

Allow 'feature' to match all features when doing stash search

Which also allows '!!feature' to exlude them
Add a comment explaining a choice of delay scale

feat: Add options to limit longwalking aka shift running

This adds a new option, longwalk_range, that gives you some control over
the maximum distance shift+direction can take you. The options are
* <constant> - a positive integer range. This is the default with the
               range being 15.
* los        - travel as far as your character's current line of sight
               range.
* visible    - travel only to tiles you can see when you start.
* unlimited  - no distance limitation (the previous behaviour).

The motivation is that sometimes longwalking can unexpectedly put you
dozens of tiles from where you started in unexplored territory, leaving
a lot of opportunity for monsters to flank you. This rather undermines
one important use case of shift+direction, which is to let the player
explore manually with significantly fewer key presses.

The constant 15 is chosen because that's a full LoS diameter normally.
With a human you'll have walked far enough that no visible tile was
visible 15 steps ago. That seems like a good default cap, without
allowing the player to walk across the entire map by accident. It's also
high enough that it hopefully won't annoy existing players who value the
unlimited range.

Committers note: reworked slightly into a single option, and added an
out of bounds check in the visible path (this did not actually have an
error case but seems better not to rely on downstream OOBs checks).

Delete some dead code

This was a no-op because all it is doing is sometimes continuing as the
last action in a loop.

Also, the check was wrong anyway, because uppercase letters come before
lowercase, not after.

Committer's note: adjusted commit message.
Do not show breath status if can't use it in current form

Create lua func you.movement_cost()

Committer's changes: To make this deterministic, we implement a
deterministically rounded version of the player's movement speed,
which we also use in a couple of other in-game displays which
could previously switch between different displays.

Make movement speed a bit less random

The calculation of movement speed has many factors. Before this
commit, these were applied with aut-scale rounding between each
step. This could lead to a player with complex speed-affecting
statuses having a variety of possible movement speeds - as a simple
example, hasted nagas could take 8, 9 or 10 auts over a move.

We address this by introudcing scale parameters to the relevant
functions, which let us do the calculations with far greater
precision and then randomly round them only once. This means that
in essentially every case, movement happens at one of two adjacent
delays.

Clean up evil conducts a little

is_potentially_evil_item covered the chaos brand, which is
already full blown evil, and chaos ammo. We make the
latter outright evil as well.

Make some forbidden items more findable

God-forbidden items were being marked as permanently useless, meaning
that by default they were hidden in ctrl+f. This makes sense for truly
irredeeemable items, but for some cases either the god or a scroll of
brand weapon may later make the item useful. In these cases, we now only
mark the item as temporarily useless, making it show up in searches.

Fix a monster lookup crash

Splinterfrost barricade lookup via ?/m crashed, since the Splinterfrost
Shell spellpower key isn't set in this case. This commit fixes this by
using a default spellpower of the splinterfrost barricade's default HD,
like we do for other forged or conjured monsters.

Fix rendering of WebTiles descriptions (Mouldy Labs)

In WebTiles, arbitrary HTML could be rendered in the browsers of all
spectators based on player item inscriptions and ancestor names that
were passed through unmodified to the description UI. This commit fixes
this vulnerability by rendering the relevant description titles as
non-HTML text. I've also made similar modifications for the description
UI of dungeon features, spells, cards, generic database lookups, game
seeds, and game versions. These shouldn't contain user-specified input,
but they don't need markup, and it's better to be safe. Probably we
should have a more systematic approach for prevening markup in
user-supplied text from being rendered by the WebTiles client.
Update the credits

Round displayed attack delay to the nearest aut

We were previously rounding down, which is both less accurate and caused
disagreements with the armour penalty (which was already rounding)

Also change the penalty scale used by the armour/shields code to match
the actual one, resolving another small disagreement here.
Don't show encumberance warnings for ineligible species

Add effect of shields to weapon delay description.

Fixes #3474

Display Status Icons on Monster Tile in Title of Describe View

The function DungeonCellBuffer::add_monster() is used once in
describe.cc to display the monster tile when viewing a monster
description, and is used nowhere else. Local Tiles has not displayed
status icons nor foreground flags on the monster tile in the title of
the describe view since commit 322ac23. A call to function
pack_foreground() was removed from add_monster() in that commit,
and that led to issue #5132.

Add a function call to pack_foreground() in
DungeonCellBuffer::add_monster() so that status icons and foreground
flags display on the monster tile in the title of the describe view.
This effectively reverts 322ac23, while incorporating the
tile_with_flags_t refactoring done in 9a5c35d, and fixes #5132.

Correct sigil of binding's description

Explain that swiftness won't effect the player

Fixes #5138

feat: add estimated XL to skill target in the skill ? menu

Because sometimes you want to know how long it will take to reach some
arbitrary skill target that isn't a weapon's mindelay or a talisman's
min/max skill.
Fix a god description typo (KSaturn)

Fix Coglin quiver switching weapons on equipment change

player_equip_set::remove() used swap-and-pop, which could
reorder the items vector. Since get_first_slot_item() returns
the first matching entry, this caused you.weapon() to return
the wrong weapon for Coglins (the only species with two
weapons), flipping the quiver display and firing target after
unrelated equipment changes like removing armour.

Use erase() to preserve item ordering instead.

Fix a constriction crash with recalled companions

When reloading a level with companions who had since being recalled, we
were killing them during unmarshalling. This meant that if a monster
being constricted was unmarshalled after its now-gone constrictor, it
was left in a state of constriction with no constrictor, which leads to
a crash.

We fix this by unmarshalling all monsters, and only then killing off the
invalid ones. We fix up old saves that unmarshalled wrongly and then
persisted the invalid state; though it's rather hard to achieve one of
these and they may well not exist in practise.

Fixes #5192
Stop messages for applying bog out of LoS

Make volcanoes roar less

Makes use of the currently unused countdown_limit parameter. Also fixed
an unpaired parenthesis.

Fixes #3858.

Fix a crash with dynamic map markers

This was typically happening in the Abyss, when the map shifted with
Malign Gateway active; the game crashed when it timed out.

This happened because map_marker::move was creating a duplicate marker
in dynamic_markers, which later caused problems in run_all.

Fixes 5280
Fix wizmode transforming into the null form

Forbid training magic skills under Trog

Some details:
* Unlike Ru-sacced skills, you keep your existing levels
* These skills are hidden from the skills menu (even if trained), but
showable on "all" in case you want to abandon.
* Gnolls continue to get an exemption from the conduct.

Also merge Trog's no-memorising and no-casting forbiddances

Don't show gaps in the show-all skill menu

Skills we don't show even in "show all" (e.g. weapons skills for felids)
were still getting their own line.

Remove some state from skill management

Previously, when we changed the player's equipment, spells, etc, we
registered our intent to show or hide a skill from the ones shown in the
skill menu, and this intent was then applied at some future time.

Instead, we just calculate which skills to show as needed (which is only
in the skill menus). Actually, we already have all the functionality to
do this, since the state we are removing wasn't cached on save.

This also removes an explicit check for skill on our active talisman -
it's a non-useless item in our inventory and is checked as such.

Remove a redundant array

can_currently_train[sk] was always equal to !is_useless_skill(sk), which
is rather easier to reason about (and is still very cheap to call).

Allow scrolls of amnesia under Trog

If anything Trog is probably in favour of you forgetting your forbidden
magic.

List spells even when none are memorisable

Now god-hated spells are not memorisable, it is important to let the
player view their library (e.g. when worshipping Trog, when it is all
useless). And this is also quite a bit clearer than useless spells just
disappearing.

Useless are listed last, so don't get in the way of comprehension.

Describe uselessness on more items

These are useful to explain the new god-forbidden items mechanic, and
also seems generally worth having.

Tweak logic for god-approved acquirement items

This is mostly a refactor to remove an unnecessary second category of
god-based hatred ("despising")

Refactor many negative conducts into forbidden acts

Following the previoius sequence of commits, most things gods dislike
are now entirely forbidden rather than merely disapproved of. We
refactor so that this split is clear and reflected in god descriptions.

Some gods still dislike targetting certain neutral or allied creatures;
this is rather harder to change because of the number of ways there are
to hurt something.

While doing this, we also reorganise Zin's conducts/forbiddances a
little. "Unclean" was redundant with "Evil+Chaotic", and is removed.
Some bits of "chaotic" (talismans, polymorph etc) made sense to sit
in the same forbiddance as "mutation", which we now call
"transformation". None of this changes the set of things that Zin
forbids.

Remove various now-unreachable penance checks

After a sequence of commits migrating penance to forbidding
items/spells, there is now no way to trigger these (and if there is, we
should fix that with forbidding the activity or item that causes it)

Forbid animating weapons hated by your god

This removes penance in favour of outright forbidding the action.

For visible monsters, you are stopped before casting.
For invisible ones, you waste a turn.

Forbid throwing items hated by your god

This removes penance in favour of outright forbidding the action.

Forbid hated spells rather than applying penance

This removes penance in favour of outright forbidding the action.

Forbid evoking items hated by your god

This removes penance in favour of outright forbidding the action.

We also start showing useless evokers in the evoke menu, as otherwise
it might be quite confusing when they disappear for god-related reasons.

Forbid scrolls and potions hated by your god

This removes penance in favour of outright forbidding the action.

The most interesting choice here is potions of mutation under Zin.
While locking in bad mutation sets until Zin's cleansing comes online
might sometimes be quite bad, removing the exception simplifies things a
good deal both in terms of player understanding and the code, and other
gods have rather harsher conducts (especially now the worst of the bad
mutations have been removed or softened).

Forbid equipment hated by your god.

This updates from gods causing penance for hated items to outright
forbidding them.

Gods will now reject your worship until you remove forbidden items and
forms. If rolled from ecumenical altars, they will forcibly remove them
from you (bypassing your form to remove melded items).

This is the first in a series of commits that will move many instances
of penance to outright bans. There are a few reasons for this:
- The rare cases that accepting penance or passively wielding a banned
item are correct are rather unflavourful.
- Penance and piety loss are confusing, and the distinction is poorly
explained (many warnings about this lie to the player).
- Codewise the current situation is quite fragile; warnings and actual
penance have to be kept in sync in many places.
Make god_hates_monster a boolean

Show useless armour in wear menu

This is consistent with weapons and jewellery, which show e.g. objects
that the species can never wear, and it can be confusing not to find
things you know you have.

Fix a few artefact properties to message on meld/unmeld

These match the mundane versions which are more accurate

Fix displaying messages when destroying melded equipment

When melded equipment was destroyed (via mutations) we were triggering
the normal "unequip" code. This printed out a bunch of harmless but
incorrect messages about the properties of the equipment disappearing.

Prevent good mutations from occasionally removing evolution/rMut

These were marked as an "inverse pair", which meant that when one
was rolled (as either a good or random mutation) it could remove
the other. This was especially visible and bad-feeling when the
player was rolling a guaranteed good mutation.

Fix an occasional misreport from mutation()

In certain conflicts with innate mutations, the mutation() function was
doing nothing but saying it succeeded, which led to some consequences
like Jiyva thinking they've given you a mutation or your evolution
counter ticking.

This does not affect the actual mutations given by the function, as we
were blocking the deletion of the innate mutation later.
Fix a build warning by making something const
Don't highlight ancestors in discord targeter (Emily Bunny)

Add rudimentary Makefile support for profiling

This commit adds a PROFILING make argument that, when defined, adds the
-pg argument for compilation and linking. This can can be used to
generate a gmon.out file suitable for analysis in gprof. I'm not going
to add any further support or documentation to this end, since I'm not
yet sure what further capabilities will be needed.

Add a dancing-related quote for Uskayaw altars

[skip ci]

Add an arachnine quote

[skip ci]

Add some quotes about insects

[skip ci]

Specify UTF-8 encoding in python generation utilities

With recent fancy glyph characters, some CI systems and servers are
failing to run these scripts. We fix this by specifying the file
encoding as UTF-8 (slightly speculatively, as I don't have access to an
equivalent system to build on), and remove the more local fix from
0a0f4f6.

Also remove some misleading messages about scripts working with python 2
(they almost certainly didn't before or after this change).

Fix a very small error in displayed to-hit chances

Maximum impact was ~1% for normal attacks and ~2% for auxes.

Fix a very small error in displayed hex success chances

The maximum impact was 1%, so similar to rounding errors in the display.

Don't crash when aiming beams at monsters with 0 EV

(Often because they're asleep.)

Fix to-hit estimate for player beam spells against monsters

The function worked by computing all possible combinations of to-hit and
evasion rolls for a given spell versus a given monster, then returning what
percentage of those combinations resulted in a hit.

However, those combinations are *not equally likely* since the range of the
second and third EV roll are directly dependent on the result of the first
roll.

This caused the UI to underestimate the player's hit chance - sometimes
very substantially. In a worst case, the game could report a ~9% chance to
hit The Enchantress when your actual hit chance was ~30% (against less
evasive targets, it might report ~85% as ~75%, which is less dramatic, but
still impactful.)

We now calculate the odds of each *initial* roll resulting in a hit, and
then average those chances (since each result of the initial roll *is*
equally likely).
Another attempt at fixing MSVC CI issues
Limit path macros to dependent objects

Potentially fix a CI issue with the MSCV build

Though I admit I'm somewhat guessing here.

Allow positive mutations while at innate stat-zero (AliasTheSpectator)

We were vetoing all good mutations if the character was at stat zero
ignoring gear; fix this so we only veto ones which reduce a stat which
is then below zero (still ignoring gear).

Make screaming refractions nonliving horrors

Their undeadness wasn't obvious (in my opinion, anyway), and undeath is not
needed to explain how someone's physical being could end up deeply muddled
due to the power of the Abyss.

Allow using the mon_glyph to remap Bai Suzhen's dragon form and SoH

The serpents of hell all shared the same in-game name, making mon_glyph
only able to remap the first of them. The same was true of Bai Suzhen's
dragon form.

Now you can do, eg:
mon_glyph += serpent of hell (coc) : ice D
if one wants.

Fix some living spells not being named properly

(Presumably reflecting an early state of development, but not even one
which ever reached trunk, I believe.)

Recolour living spells based on their spell in console

Now that there is plenty of room on the glyph.

(And change the colour of some walking tomes to better match their spell.)

Recolour derived undead based on HD instead of size

The color split was historically for small/large zombie, but size is only
loosely correlated with threat level (whereas that spriggan defender zombie
among a bunch of larger chaff is pretty noteworthy). The additional damage
from simulacra and draugr is heavily HD-scaled, after all.

The cutoff of 10 HD is fairly arbitrary, but reasonable-seeming to me.

Make merged slime creatures use a gradiant of colors in console

Instead of single subtle change differentiating '1' from all other sizes of
slime creatures, take advantage of not having many other monsters on the
same glyph now to use 4 colours instead:
 green->lightblue->lightcyan->lightmagenta

Change glyphs used for expiring clouds in console

While the progression used had a somewhat pleasing 'fading circle' look to
them, in some standard console fonts, ○ and o were nearly
indistinguishable (which has been the source of at least some player
complaints). A few of the new unicode monster glyphs are also uncomfortably
similar, so I'm trying out a replacement.

I've attempted to use characters more unlike any monster, and aimed for
something that vaguely looks like the full-duration cloud glyph (§) slowly
unravelling over time (whereas none of the ones in the previous schema
looked very much like §). Hopefully this doesn't feel unduely disruptive,
considering.

Split and rearrange many monster glyphs to make use of unicode

This commit aims to reduce both current and future overlap among many
glyphs that were already overly full (or nearly so), accounting especially
for monsters likely to be added as part of forthcoming extended reworks. I
also hope to improve clarity in a few cases where many different things
were packed into one glyph (for instance, * folded together
pseudo-firewood, projectiles, stationary things that explode, and also some
of the individually strongest monsters in the game, for a total of 24
different monsters on one glyph).

The major category changes are as follows

'h' is once more only for canines.
 -Most of its other residents are moved to 'a', which is now 'misc animal'

'9' has been split into 3:
 -'ŋ' for sapient beings (eg: gargoyles, nargun)
 -'9' for lesser golems
 -'8' for greater golems

Elves now get both 'e'/'E' (for low/high-tier elves)

Elementals have been moved to 'Σ'

Unique giants are now on 'Č'

Unique dragons are now on 'Ď'

Seraphs are now 'Å' (in anticipation of Purgatory adding 2 more Seraphs)

Jellies now get both 'j'/'J' (for low/high-tier jellies)
 -Gnolls are moved to 'ğ'

'z' is now purely for skeletons
 -Wights are moved to 'U', while death cobs are moved to 'f'
 -Floating skulls have been moved from 'z' to 'ž'

'U' is now for 'non-derived corporeal undead', absorbing all of what was
previously on 'n', plus a few more things.

'n' is now for 'nature spirit', taking dryads and eleionoma from 'R' and
water nymphs from 'm'

'R' is now more clearly for 'demonic spirit'
 -Ancestors are moved to their own glyph, 'Æ', and now each have different
  colors

'Q' is expanded to 'bird-like person', absorbing harpies and chonchon

'H' has been split into 'H'/'Þ' for non-flying, flying hybrid monsters
 (eg: minotaurs are still 'H', while sphinxes are 'Þ')

'λ' is now primarily for moths, leaving other winged insects on 'y'

'◊' is now for living/plant firewood, taking many things out of 'P' and
 a couple '*'
 -P should now contain only active plants

'I' has been split into (at least) 3:
 -'I' for proper statue monsters (eg: ice statue)
 -'‡' for other nonliving stationary monsters, such as turrets or sawblades
 -'Δ' for nonliving firewood (eg: stack of scrap, splinterfrost barricade)
  (This used to be the 'detected item' glyph, which I changed to ¿)
 -Tesseracts get their own glyph: '╬'
 -(A few instances of living immobile firewood were moved into '◊')

Ghost monsters have been reorganized:
 -'ŵ' for 'support' ghosts or pseudo-projectiles (eg: silent spectre,
  drowned souls) who lack other direct attacks (including lost souls)
 -'W' for low/mid-tier ghosts with attacks (including soul wisps)
 -'Ŵ' for high-tier ghosts (eg: flayed ghost, eidola)
 -Player ghosts have been moved to 'Z' (as a form of derived undead)
 -(Poltergeists remain on 'v')

'ω' is for tentacle (tips)

Orc apostles are now on 'ö' and are coloured according to their class

'ψ' is for 'evil person', pulling many evil mages and priests out of 'p'
 -Ragged hierophants and imperial myrmidons join them, while servants of
  whispers move to 'p'

'*' has been heavily subdivided:
 -'*' is for things that explode (eg: ball lightning, prisms)
 -'●' is for non-explosive projectiles (eg: foxfire and orb of destruction)
 -'Θ' is for actual proper monsters (eg: orbs of fire)
 -'¤' is for 'none of the above' or possibly 'floating support thing'
  (eg: battlespheres)
 -(Certain pseudo-firewood is also moved to '◊'/'Δ')

If the 'char_set = ascii' option is used, each of these Unicode glyphs will
be replaced by the nearest thematic match among ASCII glyphs. This is
somewhat imperfect in a few cases (eg: most ◊ used to be P, but some used
to be I), but maintaining a separate fallback glyph and color for every
single monster added in perpetuity seems overly burdensome given how rarely
the option is used. mon_glyph can still be used to adjust any of this to a
given player's taste.

(I realize there are quite a few outdated comments throughout the codebase
referring to old glyph categories, but some of these were already wrong for
a decade, and I'm leaving any fixups there until this commit settles, at
the very least.)

Slightly improve monster::foot_name() and hand_name()

Reduce dependence on monster glyphs (since some of those will be moving
shortly) and fix a numbers of oversights due to parts of this not being
updated since 2008 (sheep and apises and Asterion didn't have hooves,
bog bodies had claws but revenants did not - among other things).

This is still definitely wrong and incomplete, and wants a more thorough
refactoring to some form that is less guaranteed to constantly fall out of
date with new monsters (perhaps some expansion of monster shapes?), but
it's also used almost entirely for jinxbite messages and monster miscasts
(which are themselves on the chopping block), so it doesn't seem worth the
time that would be required right now.

Allow mon_glyph option to accept elemental colors

It stopped being able to in 9c2c487 but it's not clear to me why this would
have ever been a problematic case (after all, monster glyphs are the main
place we use elemental colors in the first place), and it seems to work
without issue once re-enabled.
